commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an approval runtime token option to `callGateway`, forwards it for local agent exec/plugin approval request and wait calls, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main's local agent approval request/wait path does not pass `approvalRuntimeToken`, while the gateway requires that token before treating a backend `gateway-client` as an approval runtime. I did not run a live local/web UI approval flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR has no actionable automated repair finding, but the protected maintainer label and auth-boundary surface make it a normal maintainer review/merge item.

target.title: Fix approval runtime gateway calls
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes local agent approval gateway calls so approval request/wait clients identify themselves with the approval runtime token.

## Root cause

`exec.approval.request` and `exec.approval.waitDecision` are backend gateway calls from the local agent path, but the gateway tool client was not forwarding the approval runtime token. That made approved async command completions fail the runtime authorization path in local/web UI flows.

## Changes

- forwards the approval runtime token for local approval request/wait gateway tool calls
- keeps gateway URL overrides unchanged so remote/explicit gateway calls do not receive the local runtime token
- passes the token through the lower-level gateway client options
- adds focused regression coverage for both layers

## Verification

```bash
fnm exec --using 22.21.0 pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/gateway.test.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts
```

Result: 5 test files passed, 285 tests passed.
target.author: jesse-merhi
